title: scaffold project structure
description: Create index.html, main.css skeleton, and all src/ module stubs so imports resolve.
notes: Created full project scaffold: index.html, styles/main.css, src/main.js, src/core/{state,events,tick,actions}.js, src/systems/{resources,research}.js, src/ui/{hud,buildingPanel,messageLog,researchPanel,tabs}.js, src/data/{buildings,units,techs}.js, src/utils/{logger,fmt}.js. Also created CLAUDE.md, roadmap.md, tasks.json, execution.log.

title: implement state, events, and tick loop
description: state.js (initial state), events.js (pub/sub), tick.js (setInterval 250ms emitting tick).
notes: state.js: full initial state with resources/rates/caps/buildings/units/techs/queues/messages. events.js: tiny pub/sub (on/off/emit) with Events constants. tick.js: 250ms interval loop with registerSystem(), startLoop(), stopLoop(). All systems registered in main.js.

title: implement resource system with production/consumption rates
description: resources.js system: on each tick, apply rates to state.resources. Rates derived from buildings and techs.
notes: Implemented as part of T001/T002 bootstrap. resources.js: recalcRates() sums building production/consumption with tech multipliers and unit upkeep; resourceTick() applies per-tick delta capped at caps; advances training queue. Integrated into tick loop via registerSystem().

title: implement building system (build, upgrade, demolish)
description: buildings data file, actions for build/upgrade/demolish, cost deduction, rate recalculation.
notes: data/buildings.js: 12 buildings with icon/cost/production/consumption/capBonus/requires. actions.js: buildBuilding() with 15%-per-level cost scaling, demolishBuilding(), trainUnit(). All trigger recalcRates() and emit events.

title: render resource HUD and building panel UI
description: UI components: resource bar at top, building cards panel, message log at bottom.
notes: hud.js: 6-resource HUD bar with icon/value/cap/rate, updates throttled to 1s. buildingPanel.js: grid of building cards with locked state, cost display, build/demolish buttons, click delegation. messageLog.js: scrolling event log, 15 visible entries. tabs.js: tab switcher for Buildings/Research/Log panels. researchPanel.js: tech tree with progress bar, prerequisite gating.

title: add unit data and training queue
description: units.js data, training queue system, UI panel for military.
notes: units.js: 4 units (soldier, archer, knight, mage) with cost/trainTicks/attack/defense/upkeep/requires. Training queue processed in resourceTick()._advanceTrainingQueue(). trainUnit() action in actions.js. Military panel (T007 session): militaryPanel.js added with army summary, training queue progress bar, and unit cards with cost/stat display and Train buttons.

title: implement map grid (20x20 tiles, fog of war)
description: Canvas-based 20x20 tile map with fog of war reveal on territory capture.
notes: systems/map.js: initMap() generates 20x20 tile grid with 5 terrain types (grass/forest/hills/river/mountain), player 3x3 capital at (10,10), ~20 enemy settlements. revealAround() expands fog on capture. territoryRateBonus() returns per-second rate bonuses for captured terrain (forest +wood, hills +stone, river +food, mountain +iron, grass +gold). ui/mapPanel.js: 480x480 canvas with terrain colours, player/enemy tints, fog-of-war, hover highlight for attackable tiles, click-to-attack. events.js: MAP_CHANGED, COMBAT added. state.js: map field added. index.html: Map and Military tabs added. main.js: initMap() called on boot/new-game, map saved/loaded.

title: basic combat resolution engine
description: Attack action, power calculation, outcome resolution, resource loot.
notes: systems/combat.js: attackTile(x,y) validates adjacency/fog/owner, sums unit attack values (with tech multipliers for tactics/steel/engineering), resolves combat probabilistically (win chance = power/(power+defense)), victory captures tile + grants loot + reveals neighbors, defeat loses 1 random unit. Defense scales with distance from capital (15 + dist*6). Log messages use combat-win/combat-loss types with matching CSS border colours.

title: territory capture and expansion mechanics
description: Capture adjacent tiles, apply territory bonuses to resource rates.
notes: Implemented as part of T007/T008. territoryRateBonus() in map.js returns rate bonuses integrated into recalcRates() in resources.js. Capturing tiles via attackTile() immediately triggers recalcRates() so bonuses apply. Map stats bar shows live territory/enemy/explored tile counts.

title: tech tree data and research system
description: techs.js data with prerequisites, research action, tick-based progress.
notes: Implemented as part of bootstrap. data/techs.js: 6 techs with cost/researchTicks/effectDesc/requires. systems/research.js: startResearch() validates prereqs and deducts cost; researchTick() advances queue and applies tech. ui/researchPanel.js: full tech tree UI with progress bar and gating.

title: save/load via localStorage (auto-save every 60s)
description: Serialize state to localStorage on interval, load on page open, manual save/load buttons.
notes: Implemented in main.js: _save() serializes state to localStorage key 'empireos-save'; _loadSave()/_applySave() restore on boot. Auto-save every 60s via setInterval. Manual Save button in title bar. New Game button clears save and reinits state. Version bumped to 2 to include map state.

title: tech effects applied to rates and unlock gates
description: Apply tech research bonuses to building multipliers, unit attack, and unlock requirements.
notes: Added 3 combat techs (tactics +25% attack, steel +50% attack, engineering +10% attack + unlocks Watchtower) to techs.js. Added tech requirements: knight requires metalworking, mage requires arcane, watchtower requires engineering. Wired warcraft -25% training speed in trainUnit() with totalTicks stored in queue entry for correct progress bar. buildingPanel now re-renders on TECH_CHANGED. militaryPanel reqStr shows tech icons/names via TECHS lookup. Army display shows tech-boosted combat power.

title: prestige / age advancement mechanic
description: Age system (Stone/Bronze/Iron/Medieval), unlock new buildings/units per age, prestige button.
notes: Created data/ages.js with 4 ages (Stone/Bronze/Iron/Medieval) each with productionMult (1.0/1.25/1.5/2.0), resource cost, and requirements (totalBuildings, totalUnits, territory, tech). Added state.age=0 to state.js. Added AGE_CHANGED event. Added advanceAge() to actions.js with full requirement checking + cost deduction. Applied age productionMult to building production in resources.js recalcRates(). Added age requirements to archeryRange (Bronze) and knight unit (Bronze). Building and military panels handle type:'age' in unlock logic and re-render on AGE_CHANGED. Research panel shows age section at top with current age, next age requirements checklist, cost, and Advance button. Age badge in title bar. Save/load includes age field (version 3).

title: random event system (raids, windfalls, disasters)
description: Time-triggered random events that affect resources, units, or territory.
notes: Created src/systems/randomEvents.js with 10 events (3 raids: bandit_raid/goblin_attack/pirates; 4 windfalls: merchant_caravan/bountiful_harvest/ore_vein/mana_surge; 3 disasters: drought/mine_collapse/plague). Disasters push temporary rate modifiers into state.randomEvents.activeModifiers, consumed by recalcRates() in resources.js. Events fire on a weighted-random 120–240s interval. initRandomEvents() wired in main.js boot; randomEventTick() registered as a tick system. state.randomEvents saved/loaded (save version 4). Log colours: raid=red, windfall=green, disaster=orange.

title: quest/objective system with rewards
description: Milestone objectives (first building, first unit, first capture, etc.) with resource rewards.
notes: Created src/systems/quests.js with 11 milestone quests (first_building/first_unit/five_buildings/first_research/first_capture/army_ten/ten_buildings/twenty_territory/age_bronze/age_iron/age_medieval). Each quest has a check() predicate, icon, and resource reward. initQuests() subscribes to BUILDING_CHANGED/UNIT_CHANGED/TECH_CHANGED/AGE_CHANGED/MAP_CHANGED. Created src/ui/questPanel.js rendering a card grid with completion progress bar; added 🏆 Quests tab in index.html. Added QUEST_COMPLETED event constant. state.quests saved/loaded. CSS: quest-card, quest-header, progress bar gold fill, log-entry--quest.

title: empire log / story panel
description: Narrative story panel showing empire history milestones.
notes: Created src/systems/story.js: initStory() registers dedup-safe milestone entries on GAME_STARTED/BUILDING_CHANGED/UNIT_CHANGED/TECH_CHANGED/AGE_CHANGED/MAP_CHANGED/QUEST_COMPLETED events. 16 milestone checks (founding, building x4, military x3, research x3, age x3, territory x4, quest per-completion). Created src/ui/storyPanel.js: vertical timeline with icon, title, desc, tick-to-time footer; type-based left border accent colours. Added state.story=[] to state.js (reset in initState). Included in save/load (version 5). Added '📖 History' tab in index.html. Wired initStory+initStoryPanel in main.js. Added full CSS: .story-header, .story-timeline (::before vertical line), .story-entry (icon on line, body), type-accent border colours.

title: settings panel (speed, sound toggle)
description: Settings tab with game speed slider (0.5×/1×/2×/4×) and placeholder sound toggle.
notes: Extended src/core/tick.js: added _speedMult module var; setTickSpeed(mult) restarts interval at TICK_MS/mult; getTickSpeed() returns current mult; startLoop uses _speedMult so loops started after a speed change use the right interval. Created src/ui/settingsPanel.js: speed button row (½×/1×/2×/4×) with active highlight, disabled sound checkbox with 'Coming soon' badge, About section. Added '⚙️ Settings' tab in index.html. Wired initSettingsPanel in main.js. Added full CSS in main.css: .settings-section, .speed-buttons, .btn--speed, .btn--speed-active, .settings-toggle.

title: visual polish — CSS animations, transitions
description: Resource gain/loss flash, building card pop-in, combat flash on map tiles.
notes: Added @keyframes flash-gain/flash-loss/pop-in/slide-in to main.css. hud.js: tracks _prevValues per resource; flashes .hud__value--gain/.hud__value--loss when |delta| > 40 (filters per-tick noise). buildingPanel.js: added data-building-id attr to all cards; BUILDING_CHANGED handler calls _popCard(id) which applies .building-card--popin animation. mapPanel.js: _combatFlash module var + rAF-driven _animateFlash() fades an rgba overlay on the attacked tile over ~315ms (19 frames); MAP_CHANGED listener branches on data.outcome to trigger flash vs plain re-render. CSS: .log-entry:first-child gets slide-in so newest log entries animate in.

title: responsive layout for mobile
description: Map canvas scales down, HUD wraps gracefully, panels usable on 375px width.
notes: Added @media (max-width: 480px) block to main.css: title bar condensed (font-size, padding); HUD resources shrink to flex: 1 1 30% with 10px font; tab bar uses overflow-x:auto + scrollbar-width:none (hidden scrollbar on all platforms); all card grids (buildings, units, techs, quests) collapse to single column; map canvas uses width:100%/height:auto with touch-action:none; story timeline icon offset reduced; research-active stacks vertically; age-panel tighter padding. Also enhanced existing 600px breakpoint.

title: diplomacy system (alliances, trade routes)
description: AI empires, alliance/war status, trade route resource exchange per tick.
notes: Created data/empires.js with 3 AI empires (Iron Horde/Mage Council/Sea Wolves), each with tradeGift rates and AI probability params. Created systems/diplomacy.js: initDiplomacy(), diplomacyTick() with AI stance changes (neutral↔allied↔war) and war raids; player actions proposeAlliance/openTradeRoute/closeTradeRoute/declareWar/proposePeace with gold costs (200/100/—/—/300). Created ui/diplomacyPanel.js: empire cards showing relation badge, specialty, live trade income, context-sensitive action buttons. Trade income integrated into resources.js recalcRates() by reading state.diplomacy directly (no circular import). Added DIPLOMACY_CHANGED event. Added Diplomacy tab to index.html. Save version bumped to 7.

title: seasons cycle affecting resource rates
description: 4-season cycle (90s each), rate modifiers per season per resource.
notes: Created data/seasons.js with 4 seasons (Spring/Summer/Autumn/Winter, 90s each). Spring: food +25%, wood +10%. Summer: wood +20%, food +10%, gold +10%. Autumn: food +15%, stone +20%, gold +20%. Winter: food -30%, wood -20%. Created systems/seasons.js: initSeasons(), seasonTick() advances season and emits SEASON_CHANGED; currentSeason()/seasonTicksRemaining() helpers. Season multipliers applied in resources.js recalcRates() to positive rates only (unit upkeep unaffected). Added season badge in title bar with live countdown tooltip. Added SEASON_CHANGED event. Save version 7.

title: hero unit with special abilities
description: Hero unit with unique abilities (battle cry, inspire, siege), recruit once per game.
notes: Created data/hero.js with HERO_DEF (Champion, +60 attack, 3 abilities). recruitHero()/useHeroAbility() in actions.js. Hero upkeep in recalcRates(); Inspire doubles training speed in _advanceTrainingQueue(). Combat: hero adds flat +60 power; Battle Cry ×2 attack on next strike (consumed); Siege Master guarantees win (winChance=1.0, consumed). militaryPanel.js: hero section shows recruit card (age-gated, cost-gated) or active hero with ability buttons + cooldown countdowns (16-tick refresh). army section shows hero badge. HERO_CHANGED event. state.hero saved/loaded (version 8). CSS: .hero-card, .hero-card--active, .btn--hero, .hero-ability, .btn--ability, .hero-effect--active/--cd, .mil-unit-badge--hero, .log-entry--hero.

title: leaderboard (localStorage high scores)
description: Track best territory count and gold earned per session, display top 5.
notes: state.stats = {goldEarned, peakTerritory} added to state.js+initState(). goldEarned tracked in resourceTick() (adds gold rate/tick when positive). peakTerritory updated on MAP_CHANGED in main.js._updatePeakTerritory(). _saveToLeaderboard() called on New Game before wipe; saves {name, territory, goldEarned, age, quests, tick, date} to 'empireos-leaderboard' localStorage key; keeps top 10 sorted by territory then gold. settingsPanel.js: leaderboard section renders top-5 rows in a grid table with clear button. state.stats saved/loaded (save version 8). CSS: .lb-table, .lb-header, .lb-row (--gold for #1), .lb-rank/name/territory/gold/age/date.

title: export/import save as JSON string
description: Encode state as base64 JSON, provide copy/paste import modal.
notes: Created src/ui/saveModal.js: initSaveModal(onImport) injects modal DOM + close handlers (X button, Cancel, Esc, click-outside); exportSave() builds payload, base64-encodes with Unicode-safe btoa(unescape(encodeURIComponent(json))), shows modal with clipboard copy (with execCommand fallback); importSave() shows paste textarea, decodes with decodeURIComponent(escape(atob())), validates save.state field, calls onImport callback. Updated settingsPanel.js: imports exportSave/importSave; added '💾 Save Portability' section with Export and Import buttons; binds them in _render(). Updated main.js: imports initSaveModal; calls initSaveModal(_applySave) after UI init. CSS: .modal-overlay (fixed inset, backdrop-filter blur), .modal--hidden (display:none), .modal-box, .modal-header/.title/.close, .modal-body/.desc/.textarea/.error, .modal-footer, .btn--ghost, .save-port-buttons; responsive mobile rules.

title: victory and defeat conditions with endgame screen
description: Win by reaching Medieval Age + 80 territory + 10 quests. Lose by starvation (food=0 for 30s). Game-over overlay shows stats and Play Again button.
notes: Created systems/victory.js: initVictory() resets module-level counters; victoryTick() checks win (age≥3 + tiles≥80 + quests≥10), lose (food=0 + negative rate for 120 ticks / 30s); warns at 60 starvation ticks; calls stopLoop() + emits GAME_OVER on trigger. Created ui/gameOverPanel.js: appends overlay to body; subscribes to GAME_OVER; renders win/lose box with stats (age, territory, quests, gold earned, time played), Play Again button which calls _newGame(). Added GAME_OVER event to events.js. Added state.gameOver field to state.js initState(). main.js: extracted _newGame() function (startLoop() + initVictory() + all system resets); old New Game button handler calls _newGame(); initGameOverPanel(_newGame) wired after UI init; victoryTick registered as 6th tick system; initVictory() called on boot. CSS: .game-over-overlay (fixed, z-2000, blur backdrop, fade-in animation), .game-over-box (win=gold border, lose=red border, pop-in animation), .game-over-stats (3-col grid), .go-stat-* cells, .game-over-btn.

title: resource trading market (buy/sell for gold)
description: Market system with fluctuating prices (±10% random walk every 15s). Buy/sell food/wood/stone/iron/mana for gold. Requires Market building. Market tab with price table, trend arrows, and buy/sell buttons (×10/×50/×100).
notes: Created src/systems/market.js: initMarket() seeds state.market with per-resource price multipliers (1.0), trend indicators, and totalTrades counter; marketTick() applies ±7.5% random drift every 15s (bounded 0.4–2.5×); buyPrice()/sellPrice() apply 20% spread over base gold values (food=1.5g, wood=2g, stone=3g, iron=5g, mana=10g); sellResources()/buyResources() validate Market building requirement, deduct/add correctly capped resources, increment totalTrades, emit MARKET_CHANGED. Created src/ui/marketPanel.js: locked-state card when no Market building; price table with resource name/stock, sell price (green)+trend arrow, buy price (red), ×10/×50/×100 sell (green) and buy (red) buttons; click delegation with btn--shake feedback on error. Added MARKET_CHANGED + ACHIEVEMENT_UNLOCKED events to events.js. Added state.market null field to state.js. index.html: 🏪 Market tab + panel. main.js: marketTick registered as 7th system; initMarket() called on boot + newGame; market saved/loaded (save version 9). CSS: market-locked, market-header, market-table (5-col grid), market-row, market-price--sell/--buy, trend--up/down/flat, btn--xs/--sell/--buy, btn--shake keyframes, mobile collapses to single column.

title: achievements system (persistent cross-game badges)
description: 15 achievements stored in empireos-achievements localStorage key (persists across New Game wipes). Check on game events. Show in Settings panel with icon, title, desc, unlock date. Achievements section with locked/unlocked states. Toast message on unlock.
notes: Created src/systems/achievements.js: 15 achievement definitions (founder/builder/warlord/scholar/bronze_dawn/medieval/conqueror/diplomat/champion/wonder_built/quest_master/millionaire/trader/emperor/comeback). loadAchievements() reads empireos-achievements localStorage key; initAchievements() rebuilds in-memory _unlocked Set and registers event listeners on BUILDING_CHANGED/UNIT_CHANGED/TECH_CHANGED/AGE_CHANGED/MAP_CHANGED/DIPLOMACY_CHANGED/HERO_CHANGED/QUEST_COMPLETED/MARKET_CHANGED/RESOURCE_CHANGED/GAME_OVER/MESSAGE. _checkAndUnlock(id) calls predicate then _unlock() which persists to localStorage, adds achievement log message (type='achievement'), emits ACHIEVEMENT_UNLOCKED, calls _renderer callback. setAchievementRenderer() allows settingsPanel to register a re-render hook without circular imports. Updated settingsPanel.js: imports ACHIEVEMENTS/loadAchievements/setAchievementRenderer; _achievementsSection() builds ach-card grid with unlocked (gold border, unlock date) and locked (greyed, lock icon) states; 'X / 15 unlocked' progress count; registered _renderer in initSettingsPanel. main.js: imports+calls initAchievements() on boot and newGame. CSS: ach-grid (auto-fill 220px), ach-card (unlocked=gold border+tint, locked=55% opacity), ach-icon/body/title/desc/date/locked-label; log-entry--achievement (gold border + faint gold bg).

title: toast notification system
description: Slide-in toasts for key game events (quest complete, achievement, age advance, raid, windfall, disaster). Show for 3.5s then fade out. Fixed bottom-right position, event-type colour coding.
notes: Created src/ui/toastManager.js: initToasts() creates #toast-container and subscribes to MESSAGE events; shows toast only for types in TOAST_TYPES set (quest/achievement/age/raid/windfall/disaster); showToast() creates .toast div with type modifier class, auto-dismisses after 3.5s via setTimeout; click dismisses immediately; _dismissToast() adds .toast--exiting class for exit animation then removes from DOM on animationend (with 400ms fallback). CSS: #toast-container fixed bottom-right z-900; @keyframes toast-in (translateX slide from right) / toast-out (reverse); 8 type colour variants via border-left-color; mobile shrinks to calc(100vw-24px). Wired in main.js boot sequence.

title: empire summary dashboard panel
description: New '📊 Empire' tab with at-a-glance overview: resource rates, territory, army power, diplomacy status, season, quest/achievement progress, lifetime stats.
notes: Created src/ui/summaryPanel.js with 6 summary cards in a responsive auto-fill grid. Empire header shows name/age/season badges. Resources card: all 6 with icon/name/value:cap/rate (positive=green, negative=red). Military card: unit rows with count+attack, hero row, training queue count, total attack power (with tech multipliers applied). Territory card: player tiles/%, enemy tiles, explored count. Diplomacy card: 3 empire rows with relation badge (neutral/allied/war colour coded) and trade route count. Progression card: quest progress bar (X/N + % fill), achievements bar (reads empireos-achievements localStorage, X/15), victory conditions checklist (Medieval Age + 80 territory + 10 quests) with green/red check icons. Lifetime Stats card: gold earned, peak territory, market trades, time played. Panel re-renders on 12 event types; RESOURCE_CHANGED throttled 4-tick; TICK drives a visibility-gated countdown re-render for time + season freshness. Added '📊 Empire' as first tab in tab bar (auto-activated by tabs.js). CSS: summary-grid auto-fill 230px; summary-card; sum-res-row/sum-stat-row/sum-dipl-row; sum-progress with quest/ach/victory fill variants; summary-empire-header/name/meta/badge. Mobile: single column at ≤480px.

title: wonder buildings (3 unique late-game structures)
description: Great Library (-25% research time, +1 mana/s), Colosseum (-33% training time, +3 food/s), Grand Cathedral (+8 gold/s, -50% disaster severity). Each unique (max 1 per game).
notes: Added 3 wonder buildings to data/buildings.js with unique:true, wonder:true flags: greatLibrary (🏛️, Iron Age, arcane+manaWell×2, cost 500g/300s/200i/100m, +1 mana/s, +300 mana cap), colosseum (🏟️, Bronze Age, barracks×2, cost 400g/350s/120i, +3 food/s), grandCathedral (⛪, Medieval Age, manaWell×2+wall×2, cost 600g/400s/200i/150m, +8 gold/s, +500 gold cap). actions.js: buildBuilding() rejects unique buildings when count≥1; trainUnit() chains Colosseum -33% speed after warcraft -25%. research.js: startResearch() applies -25% totalTicks when greatLibrary built; totalTicks stored in queue entry. resources.js: grandCathedral halves disaster penalty in activeModifiers loop (1-(1-rateMult)*0.5). buildingPanel.js: rewritten to for-loop; injects '🏛️ Wonders' section header before first wonder; shows 'Built ✓' badge + description for already-built unique buildings (no actions); wonder cards get .building-card--wonder gold border class; Construct button uses .btn--wonder. researchPanel.js: progress bar uses active.totalTicks ?? def.researchTicks for Library-adjusted display. CSS: .wonder-section-header (grid-column:1/-1, gold text), .building-card--wonder (gold border tint), .building-card--wonder-built (solid gold border), .btn--wonder (gold gradient button).

title: keyboard shortcuts (tab switching, pause, save)
description: Global keydown handler: 1-0 switch tabs, Space/P pause/resume, S quick-save, Escape close modal. Guard against input fields. Show shortcut legend in Settings panel.
notes: tabs.js: exported switchTab(panelId) that simulates a tab-button click programmatically. main.js: imported stopLoop and switchTab; added _TAB_KEYS map (1→summary…0→settings, -→log); _bindKeyboard() registers a global keydown handler with input-field guard and modifier-key guard; tab keys call switchTab(); Space/P toggles pause/resume (stopLoop/startLoop) with addMessage feedback; S quick-saves; Escape closes the save modal. _bindKeyboard() called from _bindControls(). settingsPanel.js: added _shortcutsSection() rendering a .kbd-grid table with <kbd> elements for each binding; section injected into _render() between leaderboard and save-portability sections. CSS: kbd element style (inline-block, monospace, bg-card, border-bottom-2px, border-radius 3px); .kbd-grid flex column; .kbd-row 2-col grid (150px keys + 1fr desc); .kbd-keys flex row with gap.

title: resource sparklines in HUD (60-second rolling SVG trends)
description: Keep a 60-sample ring buffer (one per second) for each resource. Render inline SVG polyline sparklines in each HUD resource cell showing the last 60 seconds of history. Green for positive-rate resources, red for negative. Hidden on mobile ≤600px.
notes: hud.js: added HISTORY_LEN=60, SPARK_W=60, SPARK_H=18 constants; _history object (one array per resource); _sampleHistory() pushes current value and shifts when >60 entries; _sparkSVG(values, positiveRate) generates inline SVG polyline with min/max normalisation (1px padding on all edges), flat line for zero-range data, green stroke for positive-rate resources, red for negative; initHUD() adds <span class=hud__spark-wrap id=hud-spark-{id}> to each resource div; renderHUD() updates sparkEl innerHTML; _throttledRender() now calls _sampleHistory() before renderHUD() so history is always fresh at the render boundary. CSS: .hud__spark-wrap flex align-center margin-left:2px opacity:0.75 flex-shrink:0; .hud__spark display:block overflow:visible; @media (max-width:600px) hides spark wraps.

title: enemy AI territory expansion and counterattacks
description: Enemies periodically expand into neutral tiles (every 30–60s) and occasionally counterattack player border tiles (every 90–150s). Enemy power scales with game tick. Creates defensive pressure on the player.
notes: Created src/systems/enemyAI.js with two behaviours: _expandEnemies() picks a random enemy tile, finds an adjacent neutral tile, and captures it (interval 30–60s / 120–240 ticks); _counterattack() finds player border tiles adjacent to enemy tiles and probabilistically captures one (win chance = min(0.5, enemyPower/(enemyPower+playerDefense)); interval 90–150s / 360–600 ticks). Enemy power = 30 + tick/100 so pressure scales with game age. Capital tile is immune to counterattack. On enemy capture: one player unit is lost, recalcRates() called, MAP_CHANGED + UNIT_CHANGED emitted, 'raid' log message shown. initEnemyAI() and enemyAITick() wired into main.js (registered as 8th tick system, called in boot and _newGame). state.enemyAI = {nextExpansionTick, nextAttackTick} added to state.js and initState(); saved/loaded in main.js (save version 10).

title: difficulty presets (Easy / Normal / Hard)
description: Three difficulty modes selectable in Settings. Easy: +50% starting resources, raids/disasters 40% weaker, enemy AI 50% slower. Normal: unchanged. Hard: -25% starting resources, raids/disasters 50% stronger, enemy AI 30% faster. Difficulty persists across new games. Starting-resource bonus/penalty applied only at New Game. All other effects apply immediately on change.
notes: Added DIFFICULTY_CHANGED event to events.js. Added state.difficulty='normal' field to state.js (not reset by initState — persists across new games). randomEvents.js: added _difficultyRaidMult() (0.6/1.0/1.5) and _disasterDuration() (40s/60s/90s in ticks); all raid events multiply losses by raidMult; all disaster events use _disasterDuration() and report the actual seconds in the log message. enemyAI.js: added _intervalMult() (1.5/1.0/0.7); _nextExpand() and _nextAttack() multiply their interval by _intervalMult() so Easy enemies expand/attack slower, Hard enemies act faster. settingsPanel.js: imported state and emit/Events; added _difficultySection() showing 3 buttons (🌿 Easy / ⚔️ Normal / 💀 Hard) with active highlight and descriptive text; wired click handlers that set state.difficulty and emit DIFFICULTY_CHANGED. main.js: added _applyDifficultyStart() (×1.5 or ×0.75 on gold/food/wood with floor 10); called in both the first-boot new-game path and _newGame(); difficulty saved in payload and restored in _applySave(). CSS: .difficulty-buttons flex wrapper; .btn--difficulty / .btn--difficulty-active (mirrors speed-button styling).

title: extended tech tree (6 new technologies)
description: Add Navigation (+50% trade route income, requires tradeRoutes), Alchemy (+75% mana well, +25% quarry, requires arcane), Siege Craft (+75% attack power in combat, requires steel), Fortification (-40% enemy counterattack success, requires engineering), Economics (+50% market gold, +500 gold cap, requires tradeRoutes), Divine Favor (+30% farm food, +30% mana well, requires alchemy). Wire all effects into resources.js, combat.js, and enemyAI.js.
notes: Added 6 new tech definitions to data/techs.js: navigation (⚓, requires tradeRoutes, +50% trade route income), alchemy (⚗️, requires arcane, +75% mana well +25% quarry), siege_craft (🏹, requires steel, +75% attack power), fortification (🛡️, requires engineering, -40% enemy counterattack success), economics (📈, requires tradeRoutes, +50% market gold +500 gold cap), divine_favor (✨, requires alchemy, +30% farm food +30% mana well). systems/resources.js: _buildingProdMultiplier() extended to apply divine_favor ×1.3 to farm, alchemy ×1.25 to quarry, economics ×1.5 to market (stacks on tradeRoutes ×1.75), alchemy ×1.75 to manaWell (stacks on arcane ×2.0), divine_favor ×1.3 to manaWell; economics cap bonus (caps.gold += 500) added before territory bonus; trade route loop updated to multiply by navMult (1.5 if navigation researched, 1.0 otherwise); getBreakdown() trade route section updated to match. systems/combat.js: added if (state.techs.siege_craft) attackPower *= 1.75 after existing tech multipliers. systems/enemyAI.js: changed winChance to let; added if (state.techs?.fortification) winChance *= 0.6 after initial calculation. All 6 techs display correctly in the existing researchPanel UI with no changes needed (auto-renders from TECHS data).

title: resource rate breakdown tooltip on HUD hover
description: Hover over any HUD resource cell to see a floating popup showing the full rate breakdown: buildings, territory, trade routes, season modifier, unit upkeep, and active disasters.
notes: Added getBreakdown(resId) export to systems/resources.js: mirrors recalcRates() logic to compute per-source contributions (baseline, buildings with age+tech mults, territory, trade routes, unit upkeep, hero upkeep), season multiplier as a modifier, and active disaster modifiers; returns {lines, seasonMult, seasonName, disasters, total}. Updated ui/hud.js: imported getBreakdown; added _tooltipEl module var + _createTooltip() (appends fixed #hud-tooltip div to body); _showTooltip(resId, anchorEl) calls getBreakdown, builds innerHTML with header, per-line rows (filtered <0.005), season modifier badge, disaster badges, divider, and net-rate total row; uses rAF to measure tooltip width and clamp position within viewport below the hovered cell; _hideTooltip() adds hud-tooltip--hidden class. initHUD() attaches mouseenter/mouseleave listeners to each resource div. Removed title= attr from resource divs (superseded by tooltip). CSS: .hud-tooltip (fixed, z-800, bg-card, border-h, shadow, pointer-events:none), .hud-tooltip--hidden (display:none), .hud-tt__header/row/label/val/val--pos/val--neg/modifier/modifier--disaster/divider/total; .hud__resource cursor:help.

title: map resource overlay mode
description: Add a toggle button on the map panel to switch between normal terrain view and a resource overlay that colors tiles by their primary resource contribution (food/wood/stone/iron/gold). Updates the map legend to match the active mode.
notes: Added _overlayMode boolean to mapPanel.js. Added OVERLAY_COLOR map (grass→amber gold, forest→deep green, hills→slate, river→dark teal, mountain→rust) and OVERLAY_LEGEND array. _buildHTML() now emits a .map-controls div with a '🌾 Resources' toggle button (#map-overlay-toggle). Toggle click flips _overlayMode, swaps button label to '🗺️ Terrain', adds .btn--map-overlay-active class, calls _updateLegend() which replaces #map-legend innerHTML with either terrain or resource swatches+labels. _drawTile() selects OVERLAY_COLOR vs TERRAIN_COLOR based on _overlayMode — no other logic changes needed. CSS: .map-controls flex wrapper + margin; .btn--map-overlay default (dim border/text); .btn--map-overlay-active (green border/text + faint green bg).

title: map tile hover tooltip
description: Show a floating HTML tooltip when hovering over revealed tiles on the map. Tooltip displays terrain type, ownership (your territory / enemy / neutral), resource bonus/s, defense rating, and 'click to attack' hint for attackable tiles.
notes: Added _tileTipEl module variable. _createTileTip() creates a #map-tile-tip div and appends to body (idempotent: reuses existing if already present — handles panel re-init). _showTileTip(tile, x, y, mouseX, mouseY) builds tooltip HTML: terrain name title, owner badge (blue=player / red=enemy / neutral=dim), resource bonus row (amber accent), defense value row, 'click to attack' action row when tile is attackable. Positions the tooltip below-right of cursor clamped to viewport (150×110 estimate). _hideTileTip() adds .map-tile-tip--hidden. _onMousemove now calls _showTileTip for revealed tiles and _hideTileTip otherwise; _onMouseleave calls _hideTileTip. CSS: .map-tile-tip (fixed, z-820, bg-card, border-h, shadow, pointer-events:none, min-width 130px); .map-tile-tip--hidden (display:none); .map-tt-title/.map-tt-row/.map-tt-bonus/.map-tt-owner (--player blue / --enemy red)/.map-tt-action (accent-h, bold).

title: unit experience and promotion system
description: Units gain 1 XP per combat victory. At 3 XP promote to Veteran (×1.5 attack), at 6 XP promote to Elite (×2.0 attack). XP is per unit type. Rank badges shown in military panel and army summary.
notes: Added VETERAN_XP=3/ELITE_XP=6 constants and _rankMult()/helpers to combat.js. _grantCombatXP() awards 1 XP to all participating unit types on victory; promotes rank when threshold crossed and emits UNIT_CHANGED. attackTile() applies _rankMult() when summing attack power so veteran/elite units deal more damage in combat. militaryPanel.js: added _rankMult()/_rankBadge() helpers; _armySection() shows ★/★★ badges and rank-adjusted power; _unitCard() shows rank badge + XP progress line (XP: n/threshold → NextRank). summaryPanel.js _militaryCard(): applied _rankMult() to per-unit attack power display; added _rankBadge() inline. state.js: added unitXP: {} and unitRanks: {} fields; both reset in initState(). main.js: unitXP+unitRanks saved/loaded; save version bumped to 11. CSS: .rank-badge (base), .rank-badge--veteran (blue tint), .rank-badge--elite (gold tint), .unit-card__xp (dim info line).

title: empire advisor tips panel
description: Smart advisor card in the Empire Summary tab that analyses the current game state and shows up to 4 contextual tips (warnings, info, positive). Tips cover: food/gold draining, multiple wars, age advance ready, no military, available research, resource near cap, small territory.
notes: Added TECHS import to summaryPanel.js. Added _generateTips() which checks: food rate <-0.3 (warn), gold rate <-0.5 and <200 (warn), ≥2 wars (warn), next age requirements met (ok/green), zero units after 30s (info), idle affordable research (info), any resource ≥90% cap (info), territory <6 after 2min (info). Returns up to 4 tips sorted warn-first. Added _advisorCard() which renders tips as .sum-advisor-tip divs with level modifier classes; shows '✅ flourishing' message when no tips. Advisor card rendered first in summary-grid so it appears top-left. CSS: .sum-advisor-tip (flex, border-left 3px, rounded), --warn (red tint), --info (blue tint), --ok (green tint).

title: new game wizard modal
description: Replace native browser confirm()/prompt() dialogs for New Game with a styled modal. Modal has: empire name text input, difficulty button group (Easy/Normal/Hard with descriptions), 'Found Empire' submit button, Cancel button. Also fix the duplicate id bug on the empire-name element that prevents rename from working.
notes: Created src/ui/newGameModal.js: showNewGameWizard(currentDifficulty, onConfirm) builds a #new-game-modal overlay reusing existing .modal-overlay/.modal-box/.modal-close/.btn--difficulty classes; empire name <input> (max 40 chars, placeholder 'My Empire'); difficulty button group (Easy/Normal/Hard) with live description text; '⚔️ Found Empire' gold submit button; Cancel + Esc + click-outside close; Enter in name field submits. index.html: fixed duplicate id bug on empire-name span (removed duplicate id='btn-empire-name', kept id='empire-name', added class='empire-name-btn' for cursor/hover styling). main.js: imported showNewGameWizard; btn-new-game handler calls showNewGameWizard(state.difficulty, opts => _newGame(opts)); _newGame() extended to accept optional {name, difficulty} — applies difficulty to state before initState(); updates #empire-name text after init; empire rename binding fixed to use getElementById('empire-name'). CSS: .ng-modal-box width; .ng-field/.ng-label/.ng-input/.ng-diff-desc/.ng-warning layout; .btn--ng-start (gold); .empire-name-btn cursor+dashed-underline hint.

title: multi-line resource trend chart in Empire Summary
description: Add a full-width 'Resource Trends' card at the bottom of the Empire Summary tab. Render a multi-line SVG chart with 6 independently-normalised polylines (one per resource, 60-second rolling history reused from hud.js sparklines). Include a colour-coded legend and a caption. Export getResourceHistory() from hud.js so summaryPanel.js can access the ring buffers without circular imports.
notes: Exported getResourceHistory() from ui/hud.js (returns live _history ring-buffer reference). Added RES_CHART_COLORS constant and _chartCard() function to ui/summaryPanel.js: builds a 300×80 SVG with 3 subtle grid lines and 6 independently-normalised polylines (one per resource, flat line at 50% when constant); appended to summary-grid via _render(). Added getResourceHistory import in summaryPanel.js. CSS: .summary-card--wide (grid-column:1/-1), .sum-chart__svg (bg-card border block), .sum-chart__legend (flex-wrap 11px colour-dot + label), .sum-chart__item/.sum-chart__dot (8px circle), .sum-chart__caption (10px right-aligned).

title: resource shortage alerts — HUD threshold warnings
description: Let players set a per-resource numeric threshold in the Settings panel. When a resource value drops to or below its threshold, its HUD cell pulses with a red glow. Thresholds persist across new games (user preference). Config UI lives in a new 'Resource Alerts' section in the Settings tab. Save version bumped to 12.
notes: state.js: added alerts:{} (not in initState — persists across new games). ui/hud.js renderHUD(): added per-resource alert check — reads state.alerts[r.id]; toggles .hud__resource--alert class when resource value ≤ threshold. ui/settingsPanel.js: added _ALERT_RESOURCES constant and _alertsSection() rendering a 6-cell .alerts-grid with per-resource number inputs (placeholder='off', step=10); injected into _render() between Shortcuts and Save Portability sections; bound .alert-input change handlers that write/delete entries in state.alerts. main.js: added alerts to _save() payload and state.alerts = s.alerts ?? {} in _applySave(); save version bumped to 12. CSS: .hud__resource--alert (red border + alert-pulse keyframe glow 1.4s), .alerts-grid (2-col grid), .alert-row (flex bg-card), .alert-label, .alert-input (64px number field).

title: combat preview modal
description: Before attacking a tile, show a confirmation modal with attack power, enemy defense, win probability (%), and expected loot on victory. Confirm/Cancel buttons; Enter confirms, Escape cancels. Prevents misclicks and adds tactical transparency.
notes: Added getAttackPreview(x,y) to systems/combat.js — mirrors attackTile() power calculation with no side effects; returns {valid, attackPower, defense, winChance, loot, terrain, owner, siegeActive}. ui/mapPanel.js: imported getAttackPreview; added _previewEl/_previewTarget module vars; _onClick now calls _showCombatPreview(x,y) instead of attackTile directly; _createCombatPreview() creates persistent #combat-preview overlay (idempotent); _showCombatPreview() builds modal HTML with terrain/owner subtitle, 3-stat row (attack/defense/win%), win% colour-coded green/amber/red, Siege Master and Battle Cry active-effect notices, loot-on-victory row; ⚔️ Attack and ✕ Cancel buttons; Enter/Escape keyboard shortcuts (stopPropagation to avoid double-firing); _hideCombatPreview() clears _previewTarget and hides overlay. CSS: .combat-preview (fixed overlay, backdrop-filter blur), .combat-preview--hidden, .cp-box (pop-in animation, border-h, shadow), .cp-header/.cp-sub/.cp-stats/.cp-stat/.cp-stat__label/.cp-stat__value, .cp-siege-notice (gold badge), .cp-loot-row/.cp-loot-item/.cp-loot-none, .cp-actions, .btn--cp-attack (red gradient), .cp-hint.

title: empire score system
description: Running empire score badge in the title bar, updated on all state changes. Formula: territory×10 + buildings×3 + units×2 + techs×50 + quests×100 + goldEarned÷100 + age×200. Score breakdown card in Empire Summary tab. Score column added to the leaderboard.
notes: Created src/utils/score.js: calcScore() returns total score integer derived from state; getScoreBreakdown() returns [{label, value, detail}] array for display. Formula: territory×10 + buildings×3 + units×2 + techs×50 + quests×100 + floor(goldEarned÷100) + age×200. index.html: added <span id='score-badge'> in title bar between season-badge and controls. main.js: imports calcScore; _updateScoreBadge() updates badge text and title tooltip; subscribed to RESOURCE_CHANGED/BUILDING_CHANGED/UNIT_CHANGED/TECH_CHANGED/AGE_CHANGED/MAP_CHANGED/QUEST_COMPLETED; _updateScoreBadge() called in _newGame(); score added to leaderboard entry as s.score field. summaryPanel.js: imports calcScore+getScoreBreakdown; added _scoreCard() rendering a .sum-score-rows grid with per-component rows (label, detail, value) filtered to non-zero, total row; injected after _advisorCard() in _render(). settingsPanel.js: leaderboard row replaced Gold Earned column with Score (⭐) column; lb-header updated; score formatted as s.score.toLocaleString() with '—' fallback for legacy entries. CSS: #score-badge (gold accent, pill-shape, cursor:help); .sum-score-row/.sum-score-label/.sum-score-detail/.sum-score-value/.sum-score-total/.sum-score-total-value; lb-table grid-template-columns updated for new 6-col layout (rank/name/score/territory/age/date); .lb-score gold colour.

title: research queue UI with multi-item queueing and cancel
description: Cap research queue at 3 items. Show all queued items below the active progress bar with position numbers and ✕ Cancel buttons. cancelResearch(techId) refunds cost. Queue-full feedback on startResearch() when limit reached.
notes: Added MAX_RESEARCH_QUEUE=3 export to systems/research.js; startResearch() rejects when queue length ≥ 3. Added cancelResearch(techId): removes entry from queue by index, refunds full cost regardless of progress, emits TECH_CHANGED+RESOURCE_CHANGED, logs cancellation. ui/researchPanel.js: imported cancelResearch+MAX_RESEARCH_QUEUE; replaced single-item active bar with _queueSection() helper that renders a .rq-section card with queue header (count / max), then one .rq-item per queued entry — active item shows live progress bar + seconds-left, pending items show ⏳ Pending; all items have a ✕ Cancel button (data-cancel-tech attr); click handler delegates to cancelResearch(); Research button calls startResearch() as before and now shows 'Queue full' toast on failure. CSS: .rq-section (purple border, padding), .rq-header (flex justify-between), .rq-count (pill), .rq-item / --active / --pending, .rq-pos, .rq-icon, .rq-body/.rq-name/.rq-progress-row, .rq-progress/.rq-time/.rq-pending, .rq-cancel (icon button with red hover).

title: combat history log in military panel
description: Track last 20 battles in state.combatHistory. Each entry: tick, outcome (win/loss), terrain, coords, attackPower, defense, loot (wins) or unit lost (losses). Render a Combat History section at bottom of military panel showing most recent 15 entries with outcome badges, stats, and relative tick.
notes: state.js: added combatHistory:[] to static object + initState() reset. systems/combat.js: added MAX_HISTORY=20 constant and _recordHistory({outcome,terrain,x,y,power,defense,loot?,lost?}) helper that unshifts into state.combatHistory and trims to MAX_HISTORY; called in _victory() (with lootGained object built alongside lootParts) and _defeat() (with lost unit name). ui/militaryPanel.js: subscribed to MAP_CHANGED for history re-renders; added _combatHistorySection() rendering a .mil-history card — empty state if no battles; otherwise up to 15 .mil-hist-entry rows (--win green / --loss red left border) showing ⚔️/💀 icon, outcome label, terrain+coords, loot detail or 'Lost 1 X', attack/defense stat, and relative time (Xs/Xm ago). _combatHistorySection() injected at bottom of _render(). main.js: combatHistory added to _save() payload and _applySave() (state.combatHistory = s.combatHistory ?? []); save version bumped to 13. CSS: .mil-history (bg-panel, border), .mil-hist-count, .mil-hist-entry/--win/--loss (coloured left border), .mil-hist-icon/.mil-hist-body/.mil-hist-main/.mil-hist-outcome/--win/--loss/.mil-hist-terrain/.mil-hist-detail/.mil-hist-meta/.mil-hist-power/.mil-hist-time.

title: offline progress system
description: When the player returns after ≥30 seconds of real-world absence, calculate resource gains/losses for up to 8 hours offline using recalculated rates. Apply them to resources, then show a welcome-back modal with time away and per-resource delta. No interaction required — a single 'Continue →' button dismisses it.
notes: Created src/ui/offlineModal.js: calcOfflineProgress(saveTs, rates, resources, caps) computes elapsed = min(realElapsed, 8h), applies rate×elapsed deltas to each resource (capped at caps, floored at 0), returns {elapsed, gains} or null if <30s. showOfflineModal(elapsed, gains) renders a .modal-overlay welcome-back card with time away, per-resource ±delta rows (filtered to |delta|≥1), and a gold 'Continue →' button (reuses .btn--ng-start); Enter/Escape/click-outside all dismiss. main.js: added _pendingOffline module var; _applySave() calls calcOfflineProgress after recalcRates() and stores result; boot() calls showOfflineModal after all UI panels are ready. CSS: .offline-modal-box (narrow 340px modal), .offline-header (icon + title/sub), .offline-moon, .offline-title/.offline-sub, .offline-gains flex column, .offline-gain-row, .offline-gain-icon/.name/.val, .offline-gain--pos (green)/.offline-gain--neg (red), .offline-no-change.

title: mini-map thumbnail in title bar
description: Add a 60×60 px canvas thumbnail to the title bar showing a bird's-eye view of the 20×20 map. Player tiles = blue, enemy = red, explored neutral = gray, unexplored = very dark, capital = gold. Updates on MAP_CHANGED. Hidden on narrow screens (≤600 px).
notes: Created src/ui/minimap.js: TILE_PX=3, canvas 60×60 px; drawMinimap() iterates tiles, fills each 3×3 block with CLR_UNEXPLORED/CLR_NEUTRAL/CLR_PLAYER/CLR_ENEMY; capital tile gets CLR_CAPITAL fill + 1px strokeRect border; initMinimap() attaches to #minimap canvas, registers on(MAP_CHANGED/GAME_LOADED/GAME_STARTED, drawMinimap). index.html: added <canvas id='minimap'> between season-badge and score-badge in title bar. main.js: imported initMinimap/drawMinimap; initMinimap() called in boot sequence; drawMinimap() called explicitly in _newGame() (MAP_CHANGED listener handles subsequent redraws). CSS: #minimap border, border-radius:3px, image-rendering:pixelated/crisp-edges, box-shadow; hidden on ≤600px via @media.

title: map tile improvements (farms, sawmills, quarries, mines, docks)
description: Let players build one improvement per captured terrain tile. Grass→Farm (+2 food/s, cost 40 wood+20 stone), Forest→Sawmill (+2 wood/s, cost 40 stone+30 gold), Hills→Quarry (+1 stone/s, cost 50 gold+30 wood), Mountain→Mine (+1 iron/s, cost 60 gold+40 wood), River→Dock (+1 gold/s+1 food/s, cost 50 wood+30 stone). Improvements shown as canvas icons. Picker modal on player-tile click. Tile tooltip updated. Enemy capturing tile destroys improvement.
notes: Created src/data/improvements.js: IMPROVEMENTS keyed by terrain type (grass/forest/hills/mountain/river) with id, icon, name, desc, cost, production fields. systems/map.js: imported IMPROVEMENTS; territoryRateBonus() extended to sum tile.improvement production rates after base terrain bonus (looks up IMPROVEMENTS[tile.type], matches by impDef.id === tile.improvement). core/actions.js: imported IMPROVEMENTS; added buildTileImprovement(x,y) — validates player ownership, not capital, no existing improvement, affordability; deducts cost, sets tile.improvement, calls recalcRates(), emits MAP_CHANGED+RESOURCE_CHANGED, logs build message. systems/enemyAI.js: _counterattack() clears tile.improvement on successful enemy capture. ui/mapPanel.js: imported buildTileImprovement+addMessage from actions.js, IMPROVEMENTS; added _impPickerEl module var; _showTileTip() now renders improvement icon+desc when tile has improvement, or '🏗️ Click to build X' hint when improvable; _drawTile() renders improvement icon via _drawIcon() on player-owned improved tiles (else-if chain after capital/enemy); _onClick() shows _showImprovementPicker(x,y,tile) when player-owned non-capital tile clicked; added _createImprovementPicker()/_showImprovementPicker()/_hideImprovementPicker() — idempotent modal creation, delegated click (data-imp-build attr), Escape key handler; shows info-only card when already improved, build card with cost colour-coding (green=can afford, red=cannot) and disabled Build button when insufficient resources. main.js: save version bumped to 14; formation and tile.improvement field persisted (improvement is auto-saved as part of state.map.tiles). CSS: .imp-picker (fixed overlay, z-810), .imp-picker--hidden, .imp-box (pop-in, border-h), .imp-header/.imp-sub, .imp-card (flex card), .imp-icon/.imp-body/.imp-name/.imp-effect/.imp-costs, .imp-cost--ok (green)/.imp-cost--bad (red), .imp-built/.imp-note, .imp-actions, .imp-hint, .btn--imp-build (green gradient).

title: battle formations (Defensive / Balanced / Aggressive)
description: Add a formation selector to the military panel with three stances: Defensive (-15% attack, -30% enemy counterattack success), Balanced (no modifier), Aggressive (+25% attack, +25% enemy counterattack success). Formation persists across new games. Applied in combat.js attackTile()/getAttackPreview() and enemyAI.js counterattack(). Save/load formation field.
notes: core/state.js: added formation:'balanced' field (not reset by initState — persists across new games). core/actions.js: added setFormation(type) — validates against VALID_FORMATIONS array, sets state.formation, emits UNIT_CHANGED (triggers mil panel re-render), logs message. systems/combat.js: added FORMATION_ATTACK={defensive:0.85,balanced:1.0,aggressive:1.25} and _formationAttackMult() helper; applied attackPower*=_formationAttackMult() in both attackTile() and getAttackPreview() (after tech multipliers, before hero bonus); getAttackPreview() returns formation field in result. systems/enemyAI.js: in _counterattack(), reads state.formation; if defensive winChance*=0.70; if aggressive winChance*=1.25; winChance capped at 0.9. ui/militaryPanel.js: imported setFormation; added FORMATIONS array constant (3 objects with id/icon/label/desc); added _formationSection() rendering .formation-section card with section title, 3 .btn--formation buttons (active=.btn--formation-active), .formation-desc showing active stance description; _formationSection() injected first in _render(); _handleClick() checks data-formation attr before other handlers. ui/mapPanel.js: _showCombatPreview() renders formation notice badge when formation is non-balanced (dim text, shows modifier label). main.js: save version 14; added formation:state.formation??'balanced' to _save() payload; state.formation=s.formation??'balanced' in _applySave(). CSS: .formation-section (bg-card, border, radius, padding), .formation-title (uppercase dim label), .formation-buttons flex wrap, .btn--formation/--formation-active (active=gold border+tint+weight), .formation-desc (dim 11px), @media ≤480px collapses to column.

title: per-faction enemy tile coloring on the map
description: Assign each initial enemy settlement to one of the 3 AI empires (Iron Horde / Mage Council / Sea Wolves) based on angular sector from the player capital. Tag each enemy tile with a faction property. Propagate faction when enemies expand. Clear faction on player capture. Render each empire's tiles in a distinct color on the canvas (Iron Horde=orange, Mage Council=purple, Sea Wolves=teal). Update map legend with faction swatches. Show faction name in tile hover tooltip. Update minimap to use faction colors.
notes: Added _getFaction(x,y) to systems/map.js: divides map into 3 angular sectors (~120° each) from the capital; assigns tiles[y][x].faction='ironHorde'|'mageCouncil'|'seaWolves' in _placeEnemies(). systems/enemyAI.js: imported EMPIRES; _expandEnemies() propagates tiles[ny][nx].faction = tiles[y][x].faction ?? null to newly captured neutral tiles; _counterattack() identifies attackingFaction from adjacent enemy tile and assigns it to captured tile; log message now includes empire icon+name ('⚔️ Iron Horde seized...' etc). systems/combat.js: _victory() sets tile.faction = null on player capture. ui/mapPanel.js: imported EMPIRES; added FACTION_TINT/FACTION_BORDER/FACTION_LEGEND constants (iron=orange, mage=purple, wolf=teal); _drawTile() uses faction tint+border for enemy tiles when faction known; _showTileTip() builds factionLabel from EMPIRES for enemy tooltip owner row; _buildHTML()/_updateLegend() now append faction swatches (circular .map-legend__swatch--faction) after terrain items. ui/minimap.js: added CLR_FACTION map; enemy tile color selects faction color over generic CLR_ENEMY when available. styles/main.css: added .map-legend__swatch--faction (border-radius:50% to distinguish from terrain squares). No save version bump needed — faction is part of tile data already persisted in state.map.tiles.

title: diplomatic history log in diplomacy panel
description: Track all diplomatic events in state.diplomacy.history (max 25 newest-first). Events: alliance formed/broken, trade route opened/closed, war declared, peace signed, AI-initiated stance changes, war raids. Show a scrollable history section at the bottom of the Diplomacy panel with type-coloured icons, event text, and relative time. Save/load history as part of diplomacy state.
notes: systems/diplomacy.js: added _logDiplomacy(empireId, type, text) helper (unshifts into state.diplomacy.history, caps at 25 entries); called in proposeAlliance (type='alliance'), openTradeRoute (type='trade'), closeTradeRoute (type='trade'), declareWar (type='war'), proposePeace (type='peace'), _warRaid (type='raid'), and all three _aiAction branches (type='war'|'alliance'|'peace'|'ai'). initDiplomacy() initialises history:[] on new game and adds migration guard (if (!state.diplomacy.history) state.diplomacy.history = []) for saves without it. history is persisted automatically since diplomacy:state.diplomacy is already in the save payload — no version bump needed. ui/diplomacyPanel.js: added HIST_ICON constant map; _historySection() renders .dipl-history card with up to 15 entries; each entry shows type icon, optional empire icon+name prefix, event text, and relative time (_relativeTime helper converts tick delta to Xs/Xm/Xh ago); injected into _render() after .dipl-empire-list. styles/main.css: .dipl-history (card wrapper, overflow hidden), .dipl-history__header (uppercase label bar), .dipl-hist-entry/--alliance/--trade/--war/--peace/--raid/--ai (flex row, coloured left border per type), .dipl-hist-icon/.dipl-hist-body/.dipl-hist-text/.dipl-hist-time; mobile: compact padding override.

title: mana spells system
description: 4 castable spells that consume mana: Vision (🔭, 15 mana, always) reveals 6-tile radius around capital; Blessing (🌟, 40 mana, requires arcane) +60% food+gold for 90s; Aegis Ward (🛡️, 55 mana, requires engineering) −40% enemy counterattack for 120s; Mana Bolt (⚡, 80 mana, requires divine_favor) primed guaranteed-win on next attack. Spells section in Military panel. Effects wired into resources.js (blessing), combat.js (manaBolt), enemyAI.js (aegis). spellTick() handles expiration. SPELL_CAST event. Save version 15.
notes: Created src/systems/spells.js: SPELLS catalogue (4 entries), SPELL_ORDER array, initSpells() (idempotent struct guard), castSpell(id) validates unlock/cooldown/mana/duplicate-active then applies effect; _castVision() reveals 6-tile radius around capital via MAP_CHANGED; spellTick() expires blessing/aegis at their expiresAt tick. Added SPELL_CAST event to events.js. state.js: added spells:null and barbarians:null fields; initState() resets both. systems/resources.js: recalcRates() applies ×1.6 to food+gold when blessing active (after disaster modifiers). systems/combat.js: getAttackPreview() returns manaBoltActive flag; attackTile() checks manaBolt after Siege Master — consumes it for guaranteed win (defence=0, siegeActive=true), emits SPELL_CAST. systems/enemyAI.js: winChance *= 0.6 when aegis active. ui/militaryPanel.js: imported castSpell+SPELLS+SPELL_ORDER; added _spellsSection() rendering .spells-section card with 4 .spell-card entries (locked/cost/cd/active states); injected second in _render() after formation; _handleClick() intercepts [data-cast-spell]; SPELL_CAST event triggers re-render; TICK handler extended to also detect spell CD/active activity. ui/mapPanel.js: combat preview now shows manaBoltHtml notice; ownerStr shows '💀 Barbarian Camp' for barbarian owner (T055 side). main.js: imports initSpells+spellTick; registerSystem(spellTick); initSpells() in boot+_newGame; spells:state.spells in _save(); state.spells=s.spells??null in _applySave(); save version bumped to 15. CSS: .spells-section (purple left-border accent), .spells-title, .spell-grid (auto-fill 175px), .spell-card/.spell-card--locked, .spell-card__header/icon/name/cost, .spell-cost--ok/--bad, .spell-card__desc, .spell-status/--locked/--active/--cd, .btn--spell (blue gradient), .log-entry--barbarian (for T056).

title: barbarian encampments on neutral tiles
description: Neutral revealed tiles periodically (every 45–90s) become Barbarian Camps (tile.owner='barbarian', max 5 at once). Camps get +20–35 defense and 2× loot. Visual: dark maroon tint + skull icon on canvas, distinct minimap color. Tile tooltip shows '⚔️ Barbarian Camp'. Combat preview shows 'Barbarian' owner. Capturing a camp gives the player territory + doubled loot. Enemy AI expansion can clear camps. barbarianTick() system registered in main.js. state.barbarians saved/loaded.
notes: Created src/systems/barbarianCamps.js: MAX_CAMPS=5, SPAWN_MIN=180 ticks (45s)/SPAWN_MAX=360 ticks (90s); initBarbarians() seeds state.barbarians.nextSpawnTick; barbarianTick() fires when tick≥nextSpawnTick, counts existing camps (tile.owner==='barbarian'), calls _spawnCamp() if under max; _spawnCamp() gathers neutral+revealed tiles ≥4 tiles from capital, picks from top-40% farthest, sets tile.owner='barbarian', boosts defense +20–35, stores barbDefenseBase, doubles loot (seeds from BASE_LOOT if tile.loot empty), logs raid-type message. clearBarbarianCamp(tile) restores defence and removes barbDefenseBase (used by enemy AI). systems/combat.js: _victory() checks wasBarbarian before setting tile.owner='player'; clears barbDefenseBase metadata; uses different log message '💀 Barbarian camp cleared'. systems/enemyAI.js: imported clearBarbarianCamp; _expandEnemies() now allows expanding into 'barbarian' tiles (as well as null); calls clearBarbarianCamp before claiming. ui/mapPanel.js: added BARBARIAN_TINT/BARBARIAN_BORDER constants; _drawTile() applies maroon tint+border; barbarian icon '💀' drawn before enemy sword; _showTileTip() shows '💀 Barbarian Camp' owner and loot-on-capture row; improvement hint suppressed for barbarian tiles; combat preview ownerStr shows '💀 Barbarian Camp'; _updateStats() counts barbarian camps and appends '💀 Camps: N' to stats bar. ui/minimap.js: CLR_BARBARIAN='#7a1c0a'; drawMinimap() branch for barbarian. main.js: imports initBarbarians+barbarianTick; registerSystem(barbarianTick); initBarbarians() in boot+_newGame; barbarians:state.barbarians in _save(); state.barbarians=s.barbarians??null in _applySave() (version 15 covers both T055+T056).

title: army morale system
description: Morale gauge (0–100) that drifts based on wars (-0.012/tick/war), alliances (+0.006/tick/ally), season (Winter -0.010/tick, Spring +0.006/tick), and barbarian camps (-0.003/tick/camp). Flat changes: +5 on combat win, -8 on loss, -3 on enemy capturing player tile, +10 on age advance. Combat effects: morale ≥80 → +15% attack (Inspired), morale <25 → -20% attack (Demoralized). Desertion: if morale <15, 0.05%/tick chance to lose 1 unit. Morale gauge in military panel with color-coded fill bar, tier labels, active effect line, and modifier list. MORALE_CHANGED event. save version 16.
notes: Created src/systems/morale.js: MORALE_MAX/MIN constants; MORALE_COMBAT_WIN(+5)/MORALE_COMBAT_LOSS(-8)/MORALE_TILE_LOST(-3)/MORALE_AGE_ADVANCE(+10) exports; initMorale() seeds state.morale=50 + subscribes AGE_CHANGED once (module-level guard); changeMorale(delta) clamps+emits MORALE_CHANGED; getMoraleEffect() returns 1.15/1.0/0.80 based on thresholds; getMoraleLabel() returns tier string; moraleTick() applies per-tick drift from wars/alliances/season/camps, emits on 5-point bucket crossings, checks desertion. src/core/state.js: added morale:50 field + reset in initState(). src/core/events.js: added MORALE_CHANGED:'moraleChanged'. src/systems/combat.js: imported getMoraleEffect/changeMorale/constants; applied getMoraleEffect() multiplier in both attackTile() (after formation) and getAttackPreview() (same spot); _victory() calls changeMorale(MORALE_COMBAT_WIN); _defeat() calls changeMorale(MORALE_COMBAT_LOSS); getAttackPreview() returns morale field. src/systems/enemyAI.js: imported changeMorale/MORALE_TILE_LOST; _counterattack() success branch calls changeMorale(MORALE_TILE_LOST). ui/militaryPanel.js: imported getMoraleLabel/getMoraleEffect; subscribed MORALE_CHANGED; injected _moraleSection() between formation and spells; _moraleSection() renders purple-accented card with gauge bar (color transitions red→amber→green), tier badge (5 CSS modifiers), morale/100 counter, combat effect line, and modifier pills (wars/alliances/season/desertion-risk). src/main.js: imported initMorale/moraleTick; registerSystem(moraleTick) 11th system; initMorale() in boot+_newGame; morale:state.morale??50 in _save(); state.morale=s.morale??50 in _applySave(); save version bumped to 16. styles/main.css: .morale-section (purple left-border accent), .morale-header/.morale-title, .morale-label/--inspired/--confident/--steady/--demoralized/--broken (tier pills), .morale-bar-wrap/.morale-bar (transition), .morale-value, .morale-effect/--bonus/--penalty, .morale-mods/.morale-mod.

title: war score and forced peace system
description: Per-empire warScore field in diplomacy state. Each combat victory on a war-faction tile: +5 warScore. When warScore ≥20 and player has 200 gold: 'Demand Surrender' button forces the empire to neutral and resets warScore. warScore resets to 0 on declareWar(). War score progress bar shown in diplomacy panel for at-war empires. No save version bump needed (warScore lives inside state.diplomacy).
notes: systems/diplomacy.js: exported SURRENDER_COST=200 and WAR_SCORE_THRESHOLD=20 constants; added warScore:0 to each empire entry in initDiplomacy(); migration guard (emp.warScore===undefined → 0) for old saves; emp.warScore=0 reset in declareWar() on war start; added demandSurrender(empireId) — validates war+score+gold, deducts cost, sets neutral, resets warScore, emits DIPLOMACY_CHANGED+RESOURCE_CHANGED, logs 'surrendered'. systems/combat.js: _victory() awards +5 warScore when tile.faction matches an empire at war (emit DIPLOMACY_CHANGED so diplomacy panel re-renders). ui/diplomacyPanel.js: imported demandSurrender+SURRENDER_COST+WAR_SCORE_THRESHOLD; _empireCard() builds warScoreHtml (progress bar + label + 'Surrender available!' notice) for war-state cards; Demand Surrender button rendered when ws≥threshold (red gradient .btn--surrender, disabled when no gold); _onClick() handles 'surrender' action. styles/main.css: .dipl-war-score/.dipl-ws-label/.dipl-ws-bar-wrap/.dipl-ws-bar (red→amber gradient fill, 0.4s transition); .btn--surrender/.btn--surrender:hover (deep-red gradient). No save version bump — warScore is inside state.diplomacy already persisted.

title: pause button and visual pause overlay
description: Add a ⏸/▶ toggle button in the title bar alongside Save and New Game. Add a semi-transparent centre-screen 'PAUSED' overlay that appears when the game is paused. Sync the button state when Space/P keyboard shortcuts are used. Clicking the button or pressing Space/P both toggle pause and update all indicators consistently.
notes: index.html: added <button id='btn-pause'> in .title-bar__controls before Save; added #pause-overlay div (fixed, z-500) containing .pause-box with '⏸ PAUSED' text and .pause-hint subtext. main.js: added _togglePause() which calls stopLoop()/startLoop() then _syncPauseUI(); added _syncPauseUI() which updates btn-pause text ('⏸ Pause'/'▶ Resume'), toggles .btn--paused, and shows/hides #pause-overlay + sets aria-hidden; btn-pause click handler calls _togglePause(); Space/P keyboard case now calls _togglePause() (eliminating duplicated stop/start logic inline); _syncPauseUI() called from _newGame() to reset overlay on new game. CSS: .pause-overlay (fixed inset, flex centre, pointer-events:none, z-500); .pause-overlay--hidden (display:none); .pause-box (gold border, backdrop-filter, pause-pulse keyframe 2.4s scale+opacity); .pause-hint (dim subtext); .btn--paused (gold border+text).

title: population system — citizen growth, housing cap, production bonus
spec: Add a population mechanic: state.population={count,cap}. New House building increases pop cap by 100. Pop grows when food plentiful, declines when starving. Pop contributes passive gold income and food consumption to rates. Milestone toasts at 100/250/500/1000/2000 citizens. Show pop counter in HUD.
notes: Added systems/population.js (initPopulation, populationTick, getPopulation, getPopCap). New House building in buildings.js (+100 pop cap). Population contributes +0.003 gold/s and -0.005 food/s per citizen in recalcRates(). Milestone toasts at 100/250/500/1000/2000/5000. Population badge in title bar (population-badge). POPULATION_CHANGED event added. Save version bumped to 17.

title: espionage system — spy missions against rival empires
spec: Add Espionage tech (requires tradeRoutes). State: state.espionage={cooldownUntil,log}. Three missions: Gold Heist (steal gold), Sabotage (boost morale + message), Intel Gather (reveal enemy territory). 60s cooldown between missions. 60% base success, failure triggers counter-raid. UI section in Diplomacy panel.
notes: Added systems/espionage.js (initEspionage, launchMission, canLaunchMission, espionageCooldownSecs). Espionage tech in techs.js (requires tradeRoutes, 300g/80iron/100food). Three missions: gold_heist (steal 150-350g), sabotage (+8 morale / counter-raid), intel (reveal enemy tile count). 60s cooldown. Espionage section in diplomacyPanel.js with live cooldown countdown. ESPIONAGE_EVENT added to events.js.

title: dynamic timed challenges
description: Generate one active challenge every 3 minutes with a 2-minute completion window. Five challenge types: territory (capture N tiles), gold (accumulate N gold), combat (win N battles), population (reach N citizens), mana (gather N mana). Resource reward on success. Expired challenges replace with a new one after 30s. Challenge state shown as a card at top of Quests tab with countdown timer and reward preview. Completed challenges listed below (last 5). CHALLENGE_UPDATED event. state.challenges saved/loaded. Save version 18.
notes: Created src/systems/challenges.js: 5 TEMPLATES (territory/gold/combat/population/mana), each with metric()/target(cur)/label(sv,tgt)/desc(sv,tgt)/reward(tgt) functions; initChallenges() seeds state.challenges={active,completed,nextGenTick}; challengeTick() checks completion (metric()>=target) and expiry, then generates next on schedule; getChallengeSecsLeft() for countdown. CHALLENGE_UPDATED event added to events.js. state.js: challenges:null added to static object + reset in initState(). ui/questPanel.js: full rewrite to add _challengeSection() at top (active challenge card with icon/label/countdown timer/desc/reward/progress bar, or waiting card showing next-gen countdown; completed list shows last 5 entries); TICK handler refreshes countdown every second; CHALLENGE_UPDATED/POPULATION_CHANGED/RESOURCE_CHANGED events trigger re-render. main.js: imported initChallenges+challengeTick; registerSystem(challengeTick) as 13th system; initChallenges() called in boot+_newGame; challenges:state.challenges in _save(); state.challenges=s.challenges??null in _applySave(). CSS: .challenge-section (card wrapper), .challenge-header, .challenge-card/--active/--waiting, .challenge-card__top/.icon/.label/.timer/--urgent, .challenge-card__desc/.reward, .challenge-progress with progress-bar__fill--challenge (blue gradient), .challenge-completed/__header/__entry/__reward; mobile: flex-wrap timer.

title: empire archetype selection
description: Three archetypes chosen in New Game Wizard (added as a third section below difficulty). none/conqueror/merchant/arcane. Conqueror: +25% unit attack, -10% building costs. Merchant: +50% trade route income, +1.5 gold/s base. Arcane: x2 mana well production, -25% spell mana costs. state.archetype persists across new games. Applied in resources.js, combat.js, spells.js, actions.js. Archetype badge in Empire Summary header. Save version 19.
notes: Created src/data/archetypes.js: ARCHETYPES object (none/conqueror/merchant/arcane) each with id/icon/name/desc/bonusLines; ARCHETYPE_ORDER array. ui/newGameModal.js: added third 'Empire Archetype' ng-field section with 4 .btn--arch buttons (ARCHETYPE_ORDER); click handler toggles .btn--arch-active and updates #ng-arch-desc + #ng-arch-bonuses (gold bullet lines); showNewGameWizard() now accepts currentArchetype param and pre-selects it; _submit() extracts archetype from active arch button and passes in onConfirm callback. core/state.js: added archetype:'none' field (not reset in initState — persists); challenges:null field + initState reset. core/actions.js: scaledCost() applies archMult=0.9 when state.archetype==='conqueror' (−10%). systems/resources.js: +1.5 gold/s when merchant in baseline; merchantMult=1.5 applied to trade route income loop; _buildingProdMultiplier() applies ×2 for manaWell when arcane. systems/combat.js: attackPower*=1.25 when conqueror, in both getAttackPreview() and attackTile() (after morale, before hero). systems/spells.js: manaCost computed as floor(def.manaCost*0.75) when arcane, error message uses computed cost. ui/summaryPanel.js: imported ARCHETYPES; _empireHeader() renders .summary-empire-badge--arch when archetype!=none. main.js: imported showNewGameWizard already — updated call to pass state.archetype; _newGame() applies opts.archetype to state.archetype before initState; _save() adds archetype+challenges fields (version 19); _applySave() restores both. CSS: .arch-buttons flex row, .btn--arch/.btn--arch-active (gold border+tint), .ng-arch-desc/.ng-arch-bonuses/.ng-arch-bonus-line (green); .summary-empire-badge--arch (gold pill); mobile: arch buttons column.

title: trade caravan system
description: Random merchant caravans appear on revealed player/neutral tiles every 3-5 minutes (720-1200 ticks). Caravan stays 90s (360 ticks). Offers 3 random barter trades from a pool of 8 (sell resources for gold, buy resources with gold, or mixed bundles). Caravan tile shows 🛒 icon on map canvas. Map stats bar shows live countdown. Click caravan tile to open trade picker. CARAVAN_UPDATED event. state.caravans saved/loaded. Save version 20.
notes: Created src/systems/caravans.js: SPAWN_MIN_TICKS=720/SPAWN_MAX_TICKS=1200/DURATION_TICKS=360; CARAVAN_OFFERS pool of 8 barter trades (sell food/wood/stone/iron/mana for gold, buy food/wood, buy iron+mana bundle); initCaravans() seeds state.caravans={active:null,nextSpawnTick}; caravanTick() expires active caravan when tick≥expiresAt and spawns new one when cooldown expires; _spawnCaravan() finds eligible revealed non-hostile non-capital tiles, picks random spot, shuffles 3 offers from pool, logs 'windfall' toast; acceptCaravanOffer(idx) validates resources+cooldown, deducts give/adds get (capped), logs trade; getCaravanSecsLeft() returns countdown. Added CARAVAN_UPDATED to events.js. Added caravans:null to state.js (reset in initState). ui/mapPanel.js: imported acceptCaravanOffer+getCaravanSecsLeft; added CARAVAN_TINT constant; added _caravanPickerEl+_caravanTickRef module vars; _drawTile() applies gold tint and draws 🛒 icon (overrides all others) when caravan is on tile; _showTileTip() detects caravan tile and shows caravan countdown+trade action (suppresses build hint); _onClick() checks caravan tile first; _updateStats() appends '🛒 Caravan: Ns left' when active; initMapPanel() subscribes CARAVAN_UPDATED; _createCaravanPicker()/_showCaravanPicker()/_hideCaravanPicker() create and manage the trade picker modal with 3 offer cards showing give/get resources with affordability colors, Trade buttons, live countdown setInterval. main.js: imported initCaravans+caravanTick; 14th registerSystem; called in boot+_newGame; caravans+relics persisted in _save()/_applySave(); version bumped to 20. CSS: .caravan-picker overlay, .cv-box, .cv-header, .cv-sub, .cv-countdown (gold), .cv-offers list, .cv-offer (icon+body+button row), .cv-offer__icon/body/desc/exchange, .cv-res/--ok/--bad/--gain (affordability colors), .btn--caravan-trade (gold gradient), .cv-actions/.cv-hint; mobile: flex-wrap offer row.

title: ancient relic discovery on tile capture
description: 8% chance when capturing a tile to discover an ancient relic. 6 unique relics keyed by terrain type (grass/forest/hills/river/mountain) plus 1 universal arcane shard. Each relic grants a permanent passive rate or cap bonus integrated in recalcRates(). Each relic discovered at most once per game. Toast + story entry on discovery. New Relics section in Research panel shows discovered relics with icons/bonuses; locked relics shown as '???' with terrain hint. RELIC_DISCOVERED event. state.relics saved/loaded. Save version 20.
notes: Created src/data/relics.js: 6 RELICS definitions (terra_idol/grass +1.5 food/s; lumber_talisman/forest +1.5 wood/s; mason_rune/hills +1.5 stone/s; river_compass/river +1 gold/s +200 gold cap; iron_crown/mountain +1.5 iron/s; arcane_shard/any +1 mana/s +150 mana cap); TERRAIN_RELIC lookup; RELIC_DROP_CHANCE=0.08; ARCANE_SHARD_DROP_CHANCE=0.04; RELIC_ORDER array. Added RELIC_DISCOVERED to events.js. Added relics:null to state.js (reset in initState). systems/combat.js: imported RELICS+TERRAIN_RELIC+drop chances; added _tryDiscoverRelic(tile,x,y) call in _victory() after morale change; _tryDiscoverRelic() rolls terrain-matched relic (8%) then independent arcane_shard roll (4%), skips capital and already-discovered relics; _grantRelic() records tick in state.relics.discovered[id], logs windfall message, calls recalcRates()+emits RELIC_DISCOVERED+RESOURCE_CHANGED. systems/resources.js: imported RELICS; added relic bonus loop in recalcRates() just before Object.assign — applies def.bonus.rates and def.bonus.caps for all discovered relics. ui/researchPanel.js: imported RELICS+RELIC_ORDER+TERRAIN_RELIC; added on(RELIC_DISCOVERED,renderResearchPanel); added _relicsSection() at bottom of renderResearchPanel() building a .relics-grid of 6 relic-card elements (--found: gold border, icon, name, desc, bonus pills; --locked: greyed, ❓ icon, terrain hint); relics-header shows count/total. systems/story.js: imported RELICS; wired on(RELIC_DISCOVERED,_onRelicDiscovered); _onRelicDiscovered adds milestoneId=relic_${id} story entry with windfall type. ui/summaryPanel.js: imported RELIC_ORDER; added 🏺 Relics row (X/6) to _progressionCard(); on(RELIC_DISCOVERED,_render). CSS: .relics-section/.relics-header/.relics-count/.relics-intro/.relics-grid (auto-fill 200px); .relic-card/--found (gold border+tint)/--locked (55% opacity); .relic-icon/.relic-body/.relic-name/.relic-desc/.relic-bonus (green)/.relic-hint; mobile: single column.

title: empire policies system
description: Three governance policies the player can activate (one at a time): Taxation (+25% gold, -15% food), Agrarian (+30% food, +25% pop cap, -15% gold), Martial Law (-30% training time, -8% all rates). 60s cooldown between switches. Policy selector UI in Research panel. Policy multipliers applied in recalcRates(). Morale hit on activation of harsh policies.
notes: Created src/data/policies.js with 3 policies: Taxation (+25% gold, -15% food, -3 morale), Agrarian (+30% food, +25% pop cap, -15% gold), Martial Law (-30% training time, -8% all rates, -5 morale). Added POLICY_CHANGED event to events.js. Added state.policy/state.policyChangedAt fields (reset in initState). Added setPolicy() to actions.js with 60s cooldown enforcement and morale hit on activation. Added trainMult (0.70) application in trainUnit() for martial_law. Applied policy rate multipliers in resources.js recalcRates() (per-resource effects + allRatesMult on positive rates). Agrarian pop cap in population.js _recalcCap(). Added _policySection() to researchPanel.js (policy header, intro, optional cooldown countdown, 3 policy cards with active badge, Activate/Deactivate buttons; re-renders on POLICY_CHANGED/MORALE_CHANGED). Added policy modifiers (policyModifiers[]) to getBreakdown() return value; rendered as .hud-tt__modifier--policy in hud.js. Save/load updated in main.js (version 21, policy + policyChangedAt persisted). CSS: .policy-section, .policy-grid, .policy-card, .policy-card--active, .policy-badge, .policy-icon/.name/.desc/.effects, .btn--policy-activate/deactivate, .hud-tt__modifier--policy.

title: tile fortification
description: Build defensive fortifications on player-owned map tiles. Costs 40 stone + 25 iron per tile. Adds +15 defense. Shown as a fortify option in the tile improvement picker (separate from the terrain improvement). Fortified tiles shown with a palisade indicator on the canvas. Tooltip shows fortified status. Each tile can only be fortified once.
